test.name: Verify birth fact matches resolved conflict — no-op edit
test.description: Patrick Flynn's birth fact F1 in tree.gedcomx.json already shows `place: "Ireland"`, matching the resolved birthplace conflict c_001. Tests the edit-minimality dimension: a tree-edit invocation that finds the data is already correct should make NO changes rather than 'updating' to the same value (which would touch the file and trigger spurious diffs).

input.user_message: tree-edit: confirm this fact is in the tree — Patrick Flynn's birth fact (F1) should show ~1845 in Ireland, matching the resolved birthplace conflict c_001. Check the tree and tell me whether F1 already reflects this, or whether an edit is needed.

judge_context: Should confirm that F1 already shows `place: "Ireland"` and `date: "~1845"`, matching the conflict resolution c_001 (which preferred a_002/a_009 over a_012)
Should NOT modify other persons (Thomas Flynn I2) or other facts (F2 death fact) — edit-minimality requires touching only the requested target
Should explicitly tell the user the file is unchanged rather than silently completing — the user needs to know whether their request triggered an edit
